# Terraform Azure MSSQL Management Module

 [![Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/github/release/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-mssql-management.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-mssql-management/releases/latest) [![Last Updated](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-mssql-management.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-mssql-management/commits)


Terraform module that manages MSSQL (SQL Server) databases, logins, and users on an existing
Azure MSSQL Server. It uses the `betr-io/mssql` provider for database-level management and
stores generated credentials as JSON secrets in an Azure Key Vault. Supports three connection
modes: direct Azure MSSQL Server lookup, credentials from Key Vault secret, and Hoop tunnel
(community or enterprise). Enterprise Hoop mode publishes per-field Key Vault secrets and
emits a structured `hoop_connections` output for downstream Hoop provider consumption.


---

This project is part of our comprehensive approach towards DevOps Acceleration.

It's 100% Open Source and licensed under the [APACHE2](LICENSE).







We have [*lots of terraform modules*][terraform_modules] that are Open Source and we are trying to get them well-maintained!. Check them out!






## Introduction

This module provides full lifecycle management of SQL Server logins and database users on an
existing Azure MSSQL Server. It handles:

- **Login creation** — generates random 24-character passwords and stores them in Azure Key Vault.
- **Database user assignment** — maps logins to one or more databases with configurable roles.
- **Three connection modes**:
  - `azure` — resolves server FQDN via the AzureRM provider (optionally reads admin credentials from a Key Vault secret).
  - `hoop` — connects through a local Hoop tunnel (community or enterprise).
  - `direct` — connects directly using a supplied host/port/credentials.
- **Hoop enterprise output** — emits a `hoop_connections` map ready for the Hoop Terraform provider.

## Usage


**IMPORTANT:** The `master` branch is used in `source` just as an example. In your code, do not pin to `master` because there may be breaking changes between releases.
Instead pin to the release tag (e.g. `?ref=vX.Y.Z`) of one of our [latest releases](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-mssql-management/releases).


```hcl
# terragrunt.hcl
terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-azurerm-mssql-management.git//?ref=v1.0.0"
}

inputs = {
  # ── Organisation / environment (supplied by hierarchy) ──────────────
  org = {
    organization_name = "acme"
    organization_unit = "platform"
    environment_name  = "prod"
    environment_type  = "production"
  }
  region   = "eastus2"
  spoke_def = "001"

  # ── Key Vault (required) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  key_vault_name                = "kv-platform-prod-001"
  key_vault_resource_group_name = "rg-platform-prod-001"

  # ── Connection mode: Azure MSSQL Server ─────────────────────────────
  azure = {
    enabled              = true            # (Optional) Enable Azure connection. Default: false.
    from_secret          = false           # (Optional) Read credentials from KV. Default: false.
    server_name          = "sql-prod-001"  # (Required) Short server name (without .database.windows.net).
    resource_group_name  = "rg-sql-prod"   # (Optional) Resource group of the server.
    admin_username       = "sqladmin"      # (Optional) Admin login. Default: "adminuser".
    admin_password       = "S3cretPw!"     # (Optional) Admin password.
    db_name              = "master"        # (Optional) Default database. Default: "master".
  }

  # ── Users ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  users = {
    appowner = {
      username     = "appowner"            # (Required) SQL Server login name.
      databases    = ["appdb"]             # (Required) Databases to assign this user to.
      roles        = ["db_owner"]          # (Optional) Roles. Default: ["db_owner"].
      create_login = true                  # (Optional) Create a SQL login. Default: true.
    }
    appreader = {
      username     = "appreader"
      databases    = ["appdb", "reportdb"]
      roles        = ["db_datareader"]
      create_login = false                 # (Optional) Will use db_datareader default.
    }
  }

  # ── Databases (reference only) ───────────────────────────────────────
  databases = {
    appdb    = { name = "appdb" }
    reportdb = { name = "reportdb" }
  }

  # ── Password rotation ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  password_rotation_period = "90d"         # (Optional) Rotation period. Default: "".
  force_reset              = false         # (Optional) Force password reset. Default: false.

  # ── Hoop (optional, enterprise example) ─────────────────────────────
  hoop = {
    enabled        = false                 # (Optional) Enable Hoop. Default: false.
    community      = false                 # (Optional) false = enterprise mode. Default: true.
    agent_id       = "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx"  # (Required for enterprise)
    port           = 1434                  # (Optional) Hoop tunnel port. Default: 1434.
    db_name        = "master"              # (Optional) Default DB for hoop tunnel. Default: "master".
    server_name    = ""                    # (Optional) Logical server name override.
    import         = false                 # (Optional) Import existing connection. Default: false.
    tags           = {}                    # (Optional) Hoop connection tags.
    access_control = []                    # (Optional) Hoop access control groups.
  }
}
```

## Quick Start

1. Ensure an Azure MSSQL Server and an Azure Key Vault already exist and the Terraform
   identity has `Key Vault Secrets Officer` access.
2. Reference this module in your Terragrunt stack and supply at minimum:
   - `key_vault_name` and `key_vault_resource_group_name`
   - One connection block (`azure`, `hoop`, or `direct`)
   - At least one entry in `users`
3. Run `terragrunt apply` — passwords are generated, logins/users are created, and
   credentials are stored as JSON secrets in Key Vault.


## Examples

### Azure connection reading credentials from Key Vault secret

```hcl
azure = {
  enabled     = true
  from_secret = true
  secret_name = "sql-prod-admin-creds"   # JSON secret with keys: host, port, username, password, dbname
  server_name = "sql-prod-001"
}
```

### Direct connection (non-Azure / on-premises)

```hcl
azure = {}
hoop  = {}

direct = {
  host        = "10.0.1.50"
  port        = 1433
  username    = "sa"
  password    = "S3cret!"
  db_name     = "master"
  server_name = "sql-onprem-001"
}
```

## Inputs

| Name | Description | Type | Default | Required |
|------|-------------|------|---------|:--------:|
| <a name="input_azure"></a> [azure](#input\_azure) | Azure MSSQL Server connection settings. Mutually exclusive with direct/hoop. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_databases"></a> [databases](#input\_databases) | Map of SQL Server databases managed by this module (for reference; databases are created in the provisioning module). | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_direct"></a> [direct](#input\_direct) | Direct MSSQL connection settings. Used when azure.enabled=false and hoop.enabled=false. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_extra_tags"></a> [extra\_tags](#input\_extra\_tags) | Extra tags to add to the resources | `map(string)` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_force_reset"></a> [force\_reset](#input\_force\_reset) | (Optional) Force password reset for all managed users on next apply. Default: false. | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_hoop"></a> [hoop](#input\_hoop) | Hoop connection configuration. Enterprise mode stores per-field secrets in Key Vault. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_is_hub"></a> [is\_hub](#input\_is\_hub) | Is this a hub or spoke configuration? | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_key_vault_name"></a> [key\_vault\_name](#input\_key\_vault\_name) | (Required) Name of the existing Azure Key Vault for credential and hoop secret storage. | `string` | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_key_vault_resource_group_name"></a> [key\_vault\_resource\_group\_name](#input\_key\_vault\_resource\_group\_name) | (Required) Resource group name of the existing Azure Key Vault. | `string` | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_org"></a> [org](#input\_org) | Organization details | <pre>object({<br/>    organization_name = string<br/>    organization_unit = string<br/>    environment_type  = string<br/>    environment_name  = string<br/>  })</pre> | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_password_rotation_period"></a> [password\_rotation\_period](#input\_password\_rotation\_period) | (Optional) Password rotation period for user credentials. Default: empty. | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_region"></a> [region](#input\_region) | Azure Region to deploy resources into. Example: 'eastus2', defaults to empty string as some of the resources may not require region setting. | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_spoke_def"></a> [spoke\_def](#input\_spoke\_def) | Spoke ID Number, must be a 3 digit number | `string` | `"001"` | no |
| <a name="input_users"></a> [users](#input\_users) | Map of SQL Server logins/users to create with their database assignments. | `any` | `{}` | no |

## Outputs

| Name | Description |
|------|-------------|
| <a name="output_databases"></a> [databases](#output\_databases) | Map of configured SQL Server databases. |
| <a name="output_hoop_connections"></a> [hoop\_connections](#output\_hoop\_connections) | Hoop connection definitions for Azure MSSQL. Enterprise mode only (Key Vault has no sub-key access). Community mode returns null. |
| <a name="output_users"></a> [users](#output\_users) | Map of managed SQL Server users with their Key Vault credential secret names. |
